title: Cognition OS Toolkit — CI Gate Declaration Schema
description: Single declarative source of truth for CI/CD gates. Each gate entry becomes BOTH the local run_check invocation and the CI job block, generated by gen_ci.py. This collapses the three independently-hand-edited surfaces (local CI runner script, GitHub Actions workflow, parity checker) into one file.

gates.description: The list of declared gates. Order is preserved in generated output.

name.description: Unique identifier for the gate. Becomes BOTH the local run_check label AND the CI job key — one identifier instead of three independently-named surfaces.

description.description: Human-readable description. Becomes the CI job's display `name:` field and the local section banner comment.

command.description: The local invocation. May be a multi-line string (rendered as a bash -c block). This is also the default CI command unless ci_command overrides it.

ci_command.description: Optional override of `command` for the CI-generated version, for cases where local and CI legitimately differ (e.g. verbosity flags, parallelism). If absent, CI uses `command`.

group.description: Optional grouping label used purely for section banners/comments in generated output (does not affect execution).

allow_local.description: Whether this gate is emitted into the local CI runner script.

allow_ci.description: Whether this gate is emitted into the GitHub Actions workflow.

requires.description: Tool names (e.g. docker, gitleaks) that must be present on PATH. Codegen emits a `command -v <tool> || skip` guard for the local runner and may add a CI conditional.

non_pytest.description: True if this gate's command is not a pytest invocation (e.g. a linter or static check).

sibling_test_gate.description: Name of another gate in this same file that provides pytest coverage for this non-pytest lint. Must reference an existing gate name (validated by gen_ci.py, not by the schema alone, since JSON Schema cannot express cross-array-element references).

extra_yaml.description: Raw YAML passthrough string merged into the generated CI job block, for rare CI-only needs (a postgres services block, a matrix strategy, env vars, etc). Keeps the system ~90% generated without forcing 100%.

gate._comment_at_least_one_surface: The 'at least one of allow_local/allow_ci must be true' rule is intentionally NOT expressed in this JSON Schema. Both fields are optional-with-default-true, and JSON Schema cannot cleanly express 'true after defaults are applied' with a good error message for the case where both are absent vs. both explicitly false vs. one of each. Encoding a partial version here (e.g. a bare 'not' clause matching only the explicit-both-false case) produces a confusing generic jsonschema error that shadows the precise, actionable message. This rule is therefore enforced ONLY in gen_ci.py's load_gates(), which is the authoritative validator and gives an exact per-gate error message.
